name: Jane Addams
practice: Settlement house movement, community social work, peace activism
approach: Embedded practice — live among the people you serve. Hull House wasn't an office, it was a home. The social worker doesn't visit poverty, they inhabit the same conditions. Understanding comes from shared experience, not observation.
body_of_work: Founded Hull House (1889), pioneered settlement movement in America. Nobel Peace Prize 1931. Wrote Twenty Years at Hull-House. Established kindergartens, libraries, employment bureaus, arts programs — all from one building. Proved that a single location can be a dome for an entire neighborhood.
why_instructive: She invented the idea that a building could be a whole-person support system — exactly what BATHS domes do. Her model: embed services in daily life, not bureaucratic distance.

dome_relevance.1: Advocated for labor laws, child protection, immigrant rights
dome_relevance.2: Built the prototype for multi-service integration
dome_relevance.4: Established health clinics within the settlement
dome_relevance.7: Created educational programs for immigrants and workers
dome_relevance.8: Community building as methodology, not afterthought

sphere_relevance.5: Deep demographic understanding of neighborhood needs
sphere_relevance.6: Hull House became a temporal anchor — decades of continuous community presence
sphere_relevance.7: The settlement house IS an activation — services wrapped in culture
sphere_relevance.8: Hull House persisted for over 120 years as an institution
